kill_fact: No kill_fact found. Every load-bearing figure verifies against primary sources: Q1 2026 Ongoing Adj. EBITDA $1,494M and revenue $5.64B (8-K, 2026-05-07); the $17,043M->$19,163M debt jump and $5,610M equity / ~330% D/E (10-Q, FY2026); the pending $4.7B / 5,500 MW Cogentrix close H2-26 explicitly excluded from reaffirmed 2026 guidance ($6.8-7.6B); and the Meta ~2,609 MW + AWS 1,200 MW 20-year nuclear PPAs (SEC 8-Ks). The bear's own attack vectors (merchant tail, leverage, basket-correlated single-factor AI-capex risk) are real but are already priced INTO the B/conviction-3 down-rate, not disqualifiers.
corrections: STALE/WRONG ANCHOR on the price-momentum claim: the bull thesis and Interrogator dossier assert VST is '+22% YoY' and that the AI re-rate 'has ALREADY substantially FIRED (+22% YoY, multiple expanded ~7x->10x)' and that 'market cap +22% YoY.' This is FALSE per live market data: VST is DOWN ~7.5% over the trailing year, trading $163.75 vs a 52-week HIGH of $219.82 (~25% off the high). The error cuts FOR the thesis, not against it: the central moderator objection ('the name has already run, the re-rate is spent, asymmetry is ~1:1') is materially weaker than stated -- the stock has in fact de-rated ~25% off its highs, which is closer to the 'wait-for-capitulation flush' the moderator says to wait for. The 'priced-in / already-ran' demotion rests partly on a wrong premise; the genuine entry is better than the dossier implies. Second minor correction: GAAP NI is cited as ~$1,029M with ~$723M (70%) non-cash hedge MTM -- the headline NI verifies (~$1.03B), and the ~70% non-cash characterization is directionally fair; the durable-vs-transient-EBITDA caveat stands.
evidence: Vistra Q1 2026 8-K earnings release (filed 2026-05-07, SEC EDGAR 000169281926000011): Ongoing Operations Adjusted EBITDA $1,494M, operating revenue $5.64B vs $3.93B PY; 2026 guidance reaffirmed $6.8-7.6B, explicitly excludes Cogentrix.

Vistra Q1 2026 10-Q (SEC EDGAR 000169281926000014, FY2026): long-term debt incl. current maturities $19,163M (up from $17,043M); total equity $5,610M (D/E ~330%); Lotus acquisition closed at $1,231M; $2.25B senior secured notes (Jan-2026) + $4.0B senior unsecured notes (Apr-2026) issued.

Vistra SEC 8-Ks / World Nuclear News (2025-2026): 20-year PPAs -- Meta ~2,609 MW across PJM nuclear (incl. 433 MW uprates), AWS 1,200 MW at Comanche Peak (delivery from Q4-2027, full ramp by 2032); ~3,800 MW contracted total.

Live market data (as of 2026-06-18): VST $163.75, market cap ~$55.2B, 52-week high $219.82, trailing-12-month return ~ -7.5% -- i.e. the stock is ~25% off its high and DOWN YoY, contradicting the dossier's '+22% YoY / re-rate already fired' claim.
